macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Bank of Japan Holds Rate Steady at 0.75% Amid Economic Uncertainty
items.summary: The BOJ maintained its policy rate unchanged in a 6-3 split vote, the largest divide under Governor Kazuo Ueda's tenure, signaling growing pressure to normalize policy. Governor Ueda expressed reduced confidence in the economy's outlook due to Middle East tensions, keeping the door open for a potential June rate hike despite downward economic revisions.
items.source: Central Banks Focus on Energy Shock Ahead of Rate Decisions

items.title: Federal Reserve Expected to Hold Rates at 3.5%-3.75% Amid Iran War Energy Shock
items.summary: The FOMC is anticipated to maintain its federal funds rate in the 3.5%-3.75% range, with the Iran conflict's energy disruptions dominating policy discussions. Investors are not pricing in rate cuts this year as the full economic impact of the energy shock continues to unfold globally.
items.source: Market Minute: FOMC preview and Powell's big decision

items.title: U.S. Inflation Surges to 3.3% as Iran War Disrupts Energy Markets
items.summary: U.S. inflation jumped from 2.4% in February to 3.3% in March, driven primarily by a 21% surge in gas prices following Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The OECD warns inflation could reach 4.2% in 2026 as supply shocks continue to ripple through the economy.
items.source: The U.S. Economy Was Shaky Before the Iran War. Now It's in Real Trouble

items.title: Bank of Japan Cuts Fiscal 2026 Growth Forecast to 0.5%
items.summary: The BOJ halved its economic growth forecast for the current fiscal year to 0.5%, reflecting concerns about the impact of Middle East tensions on global economic prospects. The yen initially strengthened following the decision but pared gains during Governor Ueda's press conference.
items.source: Central Banks Focus on Energy Shock Ahead of Rate Decisions

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Liquidations Surpass $245 Million in 24 Hours, Primarily Long Positions
items.summary: The crypto market saw over $245 million in liquidations within 24 hours, with Bitcoin at $121.19 million (91.63% longs) and Ethereum at $112.47 million (86.61% longs) hit hardest. This marks one of the largest recent liquidation events amid market stress.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades Down 1-2% Around $76,200-$76,764, Flashes Rare 'Morning Star' Buy Signal
items.summary: Bitcoin fluctuated between $76,425 and $78,265 over the past 24 hours, closing down 1.30%-2% at approximately $76,200-$76,764. Analyst Ali Martinez identifies a 'Morning Star' pattern on the monthly chart signaling potential upside if it holds above $73,000.

items.title: LayerZero Pledges 10,000 ETH to Support DeFi United in Kelp DAO Recovery Efforts
items.summary: LayerZero commits 10,000 ETH to DeFi United as the industry rallies to recover from the Kelp DAO security breach, which triggered a $10 billion run on Aave and raised broader DeFi security concerns.

items.title: Ethereum Drops 3.76% to Around ₹2,16,205 Amid Broader Market Declines
items.summary: Ethereum fell 3.76% in the past 24 hours to approximately ₹2,16,205 (around $2,570 USD equivalent), contributing significantly to the $112 million in liquidations dominated by long positions.

items.title: StakeStone Token Surges Over 500% to Break $1.6 in Historic Rally
items.summary: StakeStone experienced a massive over 500% price surge, shattering the $1.6 barrier in a notable project development highlighting altcoin volatility.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Taylor Swift Files Trademarks to Protect Voice and Image from AI Misuse
items.summary: Taylor Swift is moving to trademark her voice and image amid growing fears of AI deepfakes and unauthorized use. This reflects broader concerns over AI ethics and celebrity protections.

items.title: Google Announces $15 Billion AI Data Center in Visakhapatnam, India
items.summary: Google is investing $15 billion to transform Visakhapatnam into a global AI hub with a new Cloud AI data center and subsea cables. Union Minister Vaishnaw highlighted its role as a data gateway.
items.source: The Economic Times / The Week (India) / Times of Oman

items.title: Adobe Launches Firefly AI Assistant in Public Beta for Photoshop and Premiere
items.summary: Adobe has released the Firefly AI Assistant to automate workflows in Photoshop and Premiere Pro. The tool is now available in public beta, enhancing creative productivity.

items.title: AI Agent from Cursor Deletes Startup's Entire Database, Causing Customer Chaos
items.summary: A founder reported that Cursor's AI agent accidentally deleted his startup's database in seconds, disrupting customer services. This incident underscores risks in deploying autonomous AI agents.

items.title: AI Stocks Slide on Reports of OpenAI Growth Concerns; Oracle and CoreWeave Shares Drop
items.summary: AI-related stocks including Oracle and CoreWeave fell after a report raised worries about OpenAI's growth trajectory. Markets also saw S&P 500 and Nasdaq drops amid AI boom concerns.

items.title: Nvidia Hits Record High as Investors Eye AI Spending from Microsoft and Amazon
items.summary: Nvidia's stock surged to a record high amid anticipation for AI infrastructure spending updates from Microsoft and Amazon. This week is seen as pivotal for AI stock momentum.

items.title: Large Language Models Show Poor Performance in Differential Diagnosis
items.summary: Recent analysis reveals LLMs perform poorly for differential diagnosis tasks in pulmonology. This highlights limitations in medical AI applications.

items.title: US Signs Energy and AI Deals with Balkan Countries to Expand Influence
items.summary: The US has signed agreements on energy and AI with Balkan nations, widening its regional influence. This comes amid global AI competition.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: The Division Resurgence Launches on PC via Ubisoft Connect
items.summary: The Division Resurgence is now available as a free 17GB download on Ubisoft Connect for PC, expanding to all platforms including Steam. This marks a significant release for the Division community.

items.title: Nvidia Announces New GPU with Increased VRAM
items.summary: Nvidia has announced a new GPU featuring more VRAM, alongside mentions of enhanced drivers for Conan Exiles. This hardware upgrade is highlighted for gamers looking to sell old gear.

items.title: Xbox Project Helix Faces Memory Shortages Impacting Pricing and Availability
items.summary: Xbox confirms that Project Helix's pricing and availability are affected by memory shortages, yet it is positioned to lead performance in console and PC gaming.

items.title: Intel Confirms Arc G3 Series for Gaming Handhelds
items.summary: Intel has officially confirmed the Arc G3 series GPUs targeted for gaming handhelds, advancing portable gaming hardware developments.

items.title: PlayStation Mandates Online Login Every 30 Days for Digital Games
items.summary: PlayStation now requires an online login every 30 days to play digital games, with additional price increases announced in more regions.

items.title: Valve Comments on Steam Deck 2 Development
items.summary: Valve has provided updates on the Steam Deck 2, addressing community anticipation for the next-generation handheld.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: France Ditches Windows for Linux in Government Systems
items.summary: France is replacing Windows with Linux to reduce reliance on American software tools, following a similar shift in India. This move aims to enhance sovereignty in tech infrastructure.

items.title: Meta Reverses Acquisition of AI Startup Manus After China Block
items.summary: Meta is backtracking on its acquisition of Chinese-founded AI startup Manus due to a national security ban by China. The deal faced warnings amid US-China tech tensions.

items.title: Related Digital Secures Financing for $16 Billion Oracle Data Center in Michigan
items.summary: Related Digital has obtained financing to develop a massive $16 billion Oracle data center in Michigan. This project highlights ongoing investments in cloud infrastructure.

items.title: Snabbit Raises Fresh Funding at $350 Million Valuation
items.summary: Tech firm Snabbit has secured new funding from investors including Susquehanna and Mirae, boosting its valuation to $350 million. The round supports its growth in the tech sector.

items.title: Tata Electronics Head of Construction Resigns; New Executive Hired
items.summary: The head of construction at Tata Electronics has resigned, with a new executive appointed. This leadership change comes amid expansion in electronics manufacturing.
